summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened by reading Jesus's words in Matthew 24 about the signs of the end times, including wars, famines, and earthquakes as the 'beginning of birth pains.' He connected this to Paul's description in 2 Timothy 3 of the moral decay characteristic of the last days, emphasizing that the current global turmoil is a sign we are in a spiritual battle. The core message was that spiritual battles require spiritual weapons, and the sermon focused on one key weapon: the Word of God. Pastor Travis explained that the Bible is not just a historical book but a living, active spiritual weapon. He provided three reasons why God's Word is essential: it inspects us, revealing our true motives and condition; it corrects us, guiding us toward righteous living; and it protects us, as demonstrated when Jesus used Scripture to defeat temptation. The call was for believers to be deeply rooted in Scripture, to know it better than the enemy, and to use its authority in their daily spiritual warfare, living not by the world's changing standards but by God's eternal truth.
mainPoints: We are in a spiritual battle in the end times, which requires using spiritual weapons, not worldly ones.
God's Word is a living, active spiritual weapon that inspects our hearts and reveals our true condition.
God's Word corrects us according to His unchanging standard and protects us in spiritual warfare, as Jesus demonstrated.

versesMentioned.text: As Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately. 'Tell us,' they said, 'when will this happen, and what will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the age?' Jesus answered: 'Watch out that no one deceives you. For many will come in my name, claiming, "I am the Messiah," and will deceive many. You will hear of wars and rumors of wars, but see to it that you are not alarmed. Such things must happen, but the end is still to come. Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be famines and earthquakes in various places. All these are the beginning of birth pains.'

But mark this: There will be terrible times in the last days. People will be lovers of themselves, lovers of money, boastful, proud, abusive, disobedient to their parents, ungrateful, unholy, without love, unforgiving, slanderous, without self-control, brutal, not lovers of the good, treacherous, rash, conceited, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God— having a form of godliness but denying its power. Have nothing to do with such people.
The weapons we fight with are not the weapons of the world. On the contrary, they have divine power to demolish strongholds.

For our struggle is not against flesh and blood, but against the rulers, against the authorities, against the powers of this dark world and against the spiritual forces of evil in the heavenly realms.

Love the LORD your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your strength. These commandments that I give you today are to be on your hearts. Impress them on your children. Talk about them when you sit at home and when you walk along the road, when you lie down and when you get up. Tie them as symbols on your hands and bind them on your foreheads. Write them on the doorframes of your houses and on your gates.
Test me, LORD, and try me, examine my heart and my mind.
For the word of God is alive and active. Sharper than any double-edged sword, it penetrates even to dividing soul and spirit, joints and marrow; it judges the thoughts and attitudes of the heart.

In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God. The Word became flesh and made his dwelling among us. We have seen his glory, the glory of the one and only Son, who came from the Father, full of grace and truth.
Blessed are all who fear the LORD, who walk in obedience to him.
Discipline me, LORD, but only in due measure— not in your anger, or you will reduce me to nothing.
No discipline seems pleasant at the time, but painful. Later on, however, it produces a harvest of righteousness and peace for those who have been trained by it.
All Scripture is God-breathed and is useful for teaching, rebuking, correcting and training in righteousness, so that the servant of God may be thoroughly equipped for every good work.
I have hidden your word in my heart that I might not sin against you.

Then Jesus was led by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil. After fasting forty days and forty nights, he was hungry. The tempter came to him and said, 'If you are the Son of God, tell these stones to become bread.' Jesus answered, 'It is written: "Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God."' Then the devil took him to the holy city and had him stand on the highest point of the temple. 'If you are the Son of God,' he said, 'throw yourself down. For it is written: "He will command his angels concerning you, and they will lift you up in their hands, so that you will not strike your foot against a stone."' Jesus answered him, 'It is also written: "Do not put the Lord your God to the test."' Again, the devil took him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their splendor. 'All this I will give you,' he said, 'if you will bow down and worship me.' Jesus said to him, 'Away from me, Satan! For it is written: "Worship the Lord your God, and serve him only."' Then the devil left him, and angels came and attended him.

He humbled you, causing you to hunger and then feeding you with manna, which neither you nor your ancestors had known, to teach you that man does not live on bread alone but on every word that comes from the mouth of the LORD.
Do not put the LORD your God to the test as you did at Massah.
Fear the LORD your God, serve him only and take your oaths in his name.

Monday.scripture: Hebrews 4:12 (NIV) - For the word of God is alive and active...
Monday.focus: Allowing God's Word to inspect your heart.
Monday.action: Read a chapter of Proverbs and ask God to reveal one area of your thoughts or attitudes that needs alignment with His truth.
Monday.prayer: Lord, test me and examine my heart. Help me to be open to what Your Word reveals about me today.

Tuesday.scripture: 2 Timothy 3:16-17 (NIV) - All Scripture is God-breathed and is useful for teaching, rebuking, correcting and training...
Tuesday.focus: Embracing the correcting power of Scripture.
Tuesday.action: Identify one habit or thought pattern you know is not God's best. Look up a verse that speaks to it and write it down.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, thank You for correcting me. Help me receive Your discipline with humility and grow in righteousness.

Wednesday.scripture: Matthew 4:4 (NIV) - Jesus answered, 'It is written: "Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God."'
Wednesday.focus: Using Scripture as a shield against temptation.
Wednesday.action: Think of a common temptation you face. Find and memorize one Bible verse you can speak in that moment, just as Jesus did.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, thank You for showing me how to fight. Fill me with Your Word so I can stand firm when I am tempted.

Thursday.scripture: Psalm 119:11 (NIV) - I have hidden your word in my heart that I might not sin against you.
Thursday.action: Choose one verse from this week's readings to memorize. Write it on a card and review it throughout the day.
Thursday.prayer: Holy Spirit, help me hide Your Word in my heart. Let it guide my decisions and protect me from sin.

Friday.scripture: Ephesians 6:12 (NIV) - For our struggle is not against flesh and blood, but against the spiritual forces of evil...
Friday.focus: Identifying the real enemy in conflict.
Friday.action: In a situation of tension today, pause and remind yourself, 'This person is not my problem. My battle is spiritual.' Pray for them instead.
Friday.prayer: God, give me spiritual discernment. Help me to see beyond people and circumstances to the spiritual battle, and to fight with prayer and love.

Saturday.scripture: Deuteronomy 6:6-7 (NIV) - These commandments... are to be on your hearts. Impress them on your children...
Saturday.focus: Passing on the weapon of God's Word.
Saturday.action: Share one thing you learned about God's Word this week with a family member or friend.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, let Your Word flow through me. Use me to encourage someone else with the truth and power of Your Scriptures today.
